title: haplopappus whitneyi subsp. discoideus
intro: Haplopappus whitneyi subsp. discoideus, also known as the Disco Daisy, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is native to the western United States and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, with reported health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and wound healing. Haplopappus whitneyi subsp. discoideus is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in topical applications, such as salves or creams, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
